metadata.title: How to win the green market? Exploring the satisfaction and sentiment of Chinese consumers based on text mining
keywords: Consumer behaviors Online reviews Text mining Sentiment analysis LDA topic model fragrance
evaluation 8 Fast logistics and delivery logistics
you get what you pay for
product quality Negative 1 Poor service attitude customer service

abstract: In order to fully understand the overall evaluations and sentiments of green products by the Chinese public, and comprehensively enhance the public's awareness of green consumption, this paper conducts sentiment analysis and text mining based on 85306 online reviews of 8 typical green products in four major fields of China's JingDong (JD.com) e-commerce platform, including organic food, daily cleaning, green building materials, and energy-saving home appliances. Research has found that: (1) The consumers' preferences and attentions to different attributes of green products vary depending on the product; (2) The public's overall evaluations and satisfactions with existing products are relatively high; (3) Found 9 topics related to consumers' positive sentiments (i.e., enthusiastic service, beautiful appearance, good taste, good quality, favorable price, trustworthy brand, powerful function and effect, fast logistics and delivery, high-cost performance) and 3 topics related to negative sentiments (i.e., poor service attitude, smelly, and noise); (4) Consumers' attentions, positive and negative sentiments for most of the attributes of this green product show an upward trend to varying degrees. The results of this paper provide reliable practical evidence for the promotion and use of green products, which is of great significance for promoting green consumption.

paragraphs: For a long time, economic prosperity and ecological environment protection have been considered two issues that cannot be balanced (Tallis et al., 2008). Faced with the dual pressures of resource constraints and environmental protection, governments around the world are actively exploring and boldly practicing new development methods. The emergence of green consumption concept provides an effective solution for coordinating the balance between sustainable economic development and ecological environment protection (Tezer & Bodur, 2020). For this reason, the Chinese government has successively issued a series intervention and guidance policies of green consumption, and has made positive progress in promoting green consumption (Bo & Zhuang, 2022;Zhuang, 2019). The China public's consumption philosophy and lifestyle are gradually moving towards a more positive, healthy, and eco-friendly direction (Brzustewicz & Singh, 2021;Sun et al., 2021). However, in the specific implementation process, there is still a certain gap between the actual performance of the China public and ideal expectations (Dong et al., 2022). In certain specific fields, the public's green consumption behavior exhibits a phenomenon of "high cognition and low practice" (Policy Research Center for Environment and Economy, Ministry of Ecology and environment of the People's Republic of China, 2021). Relevant scholars' researches also confirm this point. They further point out that there are various problems in the field of green consumption in China, e.g., low purchasing power, asymmetric product information, insufficient effective supply, and poor circulation channels, which seriously restrict the development of China's green consumption market (Zhou & Shi, 2022). Therefore, further research on China's green consumption, especially the purchase of green products on the demand side of daily life, is of great significance for further promoting China's sustainable development and global carbon reduction processes in the world.

Recently, green consumption has sparked joint discussions in various fields, e.g., economy, management, environment, behavior, psychology, and marketing (Huang, Long, Chen, Li, et al., 2022). In early studies, these scholars focused on the researches of green consumers (Connolly & Prothero, 2008). They are committed to analyzing which consumers are more inclined towards green consumption by comparing differences in demographic factors (i.e., gender, age, education level, income level, etc.) (Peattie, 2010). Subsequently, scholars' focus gradually shifts to individual behavioral psychological factors and external situational factors. They believe that individual behavioral and psychological factors (e.g., subjective norms (Roh et al., 2022), environmental concern (Mohd et al., 2021), environmental attitude (Paul et al., 2016), environmental knowledge (Pagiaslis & Krontalis, 2014), etc.) and external situational factors (e.g., green marketing (Rustam et al., 2020), family environment (Ding et al., 2018), social atmosphere (Li & Shao, 2020), and social media (Shen et al., 2023), etc.) seem to be more effective in measuring and predicting green consumption behaviors. At the same time, some scholars have begun to focus on green products. They regard green consumption behaviors as comprehensive consideration and balance between green attributes (i.e., eco-friendly, energy conservation, organic, and healthy etc.) and other non-green attributes (i.e., price, function, packaging, appearance, and quality, etc.) (Luchs & Kumar, 2017). For example, these scholars believe that the quality and effectiveness of green products often have a direct impact on consumer purchasing decisions (Newman et al., 2014). Similarly, the price of green products sometimes hinders the occurrence of green consumption behaviors (Zhang et al., 2018). A study showed that a considerable number of consumers are unwilling to pay a premium for the green attributes of green products (Yang et al., 2021).

Sentiment is an important component of consumer perceived value. It reflects the comparison and sentiment reactions between the public's perception of product and service attributes and the ultimate value acquisition (Dubé & Menon, 2000), which refers to a series of psychological evaluations generated by consumers during the process of purchasing products and enjoying services. Numerous studies have confirmed the potential impact of sentimental factors on behavior shaping (Ruiz-Mafe et al., 2018). According to the cognitive affective model, consumers' purchasing decisions and behaviors are influenced by both cognitive and sentiment factors (Suwelack et al., 2011). For example, positive sentiments can promote consumers to form a good understanding of products and services, thereby promoting the occurrence of purchasing behavior (Septianto & Chiew, 2018). Previous studies have shown that sentiment factors that stimulate consumers, especially positive sentiments, are conducive to promoting the occurrence of green consumption behavior (Wang & Wu, 2016). The potential impact of micro individual sentimental responses on shaping green consumption behavior may provide new directions and ideas for current research (Peattie, 2010). But it seems to have been largely neglected in previous literatures (Ye, 2019).

Promoting green products effectively not only requires certain subsidies and support for the production end, but also requires supervision of the sentiment orientation of the demand side, to improve consumers' green consumption awareness and sentimental identity (Yang et al., 2022). However, existing researches have failed to pay good attentions to the consumer sentiments in the process of green consumption (Suphasomboon & Vassanadumrongdee, 2022) and the attributes of green products (Jo & Shin, 2017). These studies rarely conduct sentiment analysis on online reviews of green products, and mainly focus on specific green products (e.g., organic food or energy-saving appliances (Malekpour Koupaei et al., 2020;Singh & Glińska-Neweś, 2022). It is not possible to balance different green products and green attributes. Especially, most studies emphasize the importance of sentiments in shaping behavior (Sun et al., 2021), but few have specifically and explicitly informed us about the true sentiments and evaluations of these green products among the Chinese consumers. And how do consumers of different products weigh these product attributes (Dinh et al., 2021)? What factors affect the evaluations and sentiments of the Chinese consumer towards green products? In addition, previous research on green consumption mainly obtained data in the form of questionnaires and interviews (Ding et al., 2018;Li & Shao, 2020), due to privacy and confidentiality needs, the authenticity of the data is sometimes questionable.

Therefore, compared to previous research, the significance of this paper is mainly reflected in the following aspects:(1) This paper selects 8 typical green products from the fields of organic food, daily cleaning, green building materials, and energy-saving appliances that residents consume in daily life. This not only effectively balances different categories of green products, but also helps to reveal the overall evaluations and preferences of the public towards existing green products more comprehensively. In addition, it also can help marketing staffs better understand which attributes are important to Chinese consumers, which attributes affect their sentiments towards green products. (2) This paper uses web crawler technology to collect online reviews data generated by users of e-commerce platforms, which is more authentic and comprehensive (Rese et al., 2014), enabling this paper to conduct large-scale data driven exploration of the time, space, and content of public consumption sentiments (Danner & Menapace, 2020;Wei et al., 2021). It can not only help us to better identify the changes in Chinese public sentiments towards green products, but also further enrich the data sources and methods of green consumption research (Malekpour Koupaei et al., 2020).

(3) This study uses real consumption data of green products on Chinese e-commerce platforms as research samples to construct an online reviews corpus for green products. This provides directional guidance and reliable practical evidence for enterprises to carry out production innovation, promote the promotion and use of green products in real life, and provides some reference for other countries to carry out green consumption related research.

sections.header_raw: The concept and connotation of green products
sections.header_norm: the concept and connotation of green products
paragraphs: Green products, usually refer to products that use fewer natural resources and have less negative impact on the environment during production, manufacturing, purchase, use, and disposal processes, and meet environmental standards (Luchs et al., 2010). It takes protecting of the natural environment as the starting point and have the characteristics of resource conservation, non-pollution, and renewable (Cai et al., 2017). In relevant research, scholars have defined the concept and connotation of green products from multiple dimensions (Dangelico & Pontrandolfo, 2010). Firstly, from the perspective of product life cycle, whether a product is a green product should be based on the evaluations of its impact on environmental emissions of air, soil or water and issues related to energy consumption in each specific link of the process of product composition, source, production, transportation, actual consumption, and disposal (Biswas & Roy, 2015); Secondly, from the perspective of consumer perception, green products should be able to give consumers a positive green perception, i.e., products that consumers subjectively perceive as having green attributes, e.g., environmental protection, energy conservation, resource conservation, and beneficial to the environment and human health (Zhang et al., 2018); Finally, from the perspective of technical certification, green products need to pass the environmental certification of relevant departments or be marked with corresponding green environmental protection label (Fraccascia et al., 2018). In our daily life, the range of green products is very wide, including organic food, phosphorus-free detergents, energy-saving LED lights, energy-saving lamps, home lighting, green building materials, energy-saving appliances, new energy vehicles, etc. Products with characteristics, e.g., pollution-free, noise-free, recyclable, energy saving, eco-friendly, and beneficial to human health and safety can all be called green products. And the green attribute refers to the characteristics or manifestations of "green" in the components of these green products (Skard et al., 2021), i.e., energy-saving, recycling, organic, noise-free, and healthy features that can promote the efficient utilization of ecological resources and increase personal and social ecological benefits; Or low-carbon, biodegradable, clean and pollution-free features that can reduce greenhouse gas emissions, avoid the generation of environment pollution and pollutant waste, and reduce personal and social ecological losses (Gong & Sheng, 2023;Marcon et al., 2022).

paragraphs: When the consumers purchase green products, they often involve a comprehensive balance and selection between personal preferences and the different attributes of green products (Luchs & Kumar, 2017;Schuitema & de Groot, 2015). That is, consumers will make the final consumption decision by perceiving whether the product attributes meet their personal preferences (Hahnel et al., 2014). Therefore, the attributes of the green product are also key factors affecting green consumption (Joshi & Rahman, 2015), e.g., prices (Singh & Verma, 2017;Zhang et al., 2018), product types (Liobikien ė & Bernatonien ė, 2017), brand (Ryan & Casidy, 2018), ecological label information (Cai et al., 2017), and its functional, social, and sentimental values (Mutum et al., 2020), may all affect consumers' final decisions. In recent years, due to the advantages of green products in health, energy conservation, and eco-friendly (Singh & Verma, 2017), consumer purchasing decisions have shifted towards green products (Paul et al., 2016). However, in actual purchasing procedures, this is not the case (Nguyen et al., 2019). The green attributes of green products are often neglected by consumers and constantly placed in a more backward position (Schuitema & de Groot, 2015).

On the one hand, the green characteristics or attributes attached to green products (e.g., energy-saving, eco-friendly, resource conservation, and benefits to human health) generally lead to higher prices for green products than ordinary products (Peattie, 2010). But the savings in living costs and improvements in the natural environment that it brings often take some time to be reflected. Especially for some altruistic green products, their additional value is reflected in the improvement of the overall natural environment rather than direct and immediate feedback to consumers (Schuitema & de Groot, 2015), which often hinders the emergence of green consumption behavior to some extent. Therefore, although green products have higher value returns in the long run, a large proportion of consumers still choose ordinary products due to immediate cost savings (Joshi & Rahman, 2015). On the other hand, due to immature production technology, green products have not achieved significant improvement in quality. Previous researches have shown that consumers generally believe that when companies make progress in improving environmental benefits of the products, resources may have shifted from other attributes. In other words, the acquisition of green attributes also implies sacrifices and compromises in other attributes, e. g., price and functionality (Newman et al., 2014). In addition, for some products, their green technology innovation does not revolve around the core functions of the products (Gershoff & Frels, 2015), which creates certain difficulties for consumers to identify and perceive the green attributes of the products, leading to a general recognition that the functional attributes of green products have not significantly improved compared to traditional products. Therefore, consumers often switch to traditional products due to the lack of functionality and quality of green products in actual purchasing procedures (Pancer et al., 2017;Skard et al., 2021).

paragraphs: Consumer satisfaction is a subjective evaluation obtained by consumers based on the comparison of expected quality and perceived quality of products or services. It reflects the actual effect of products or services provided by enterprises on meeting consumer needs. When consumers' expectations are fully met, they will feel satisfied. On the contrary, when there is a significant gap between the actual situation and consumer expectations, consumers will feel dissatisfied. Consumers' satisfaction of green products reflects the degree to which a product or service meets consumers' sustainable expectations and green needs (Chen, 2010). It is not only a prerequisite for consumers to trust green products and form brand loyalty, but also an important predictive indicator of green consumption behavior (Lakatos et al., 2021). In a word, the higher the satisfaction of green consumers, the more likely they are to trust the product and recommend it to others. In addition, previous studies have shown a close relationship between green satisfaction and the sentimental, green, and social values of green products (Issock Issock et al., 2019). This means that companies can enhance consumers' sentiments and satisfactions with current products and services by improving their performance in green and environmental protection, thereby increasing consumers' willingness to repeat consumption (Román-Augusto et al., 2022). Therefore, a thorough understanding of the public's views, evaluations, and satisfaction with green products can help better assist governments or businesses in acting, thereby effectively promoting green product consumption (Lakatos et al., 2021).

sections.paper_id: Li et al. - 2023 - How to win the green market Exploring the satisfaction and sentiment of Chinese consumers based on
paragraphs: This study aims to explore the sentiments and overall satisfactions of Chinese consumers towards existing green products by mining and analyzing online reviews of typical green products using sentiment analysis techniques. According to previous studies, consumers' green consumption decisions involve personal preferences and the selection and trade-off of different product attributes (Luchs & Kumar, 2017), and these studies have well focused on the trade-offs between certain attributes of consumers, e.g., green attributes and price (Qi & Ploeger, 2021). However, in real-life consumption scenarios, consumers face a much more complex decision than simply sorting between two or three specific attributes. Even for the same attribute (i.e., green), there are still significant differences in the importance of different green product consumption decisions (Gong et al., 2022). Therefore, deepening our understanding of which product attributes consumers value more is of great significance. But our understanding of this is still very limited (Marcon et al., 2022). Secondly, consumer satisfactions are significantly influenced by multiple product attributes (Wang et al., 2018). Some studies suggest that the green attributes of products can trigger consumers' analysis of benefits and encourage them to develop positive purchasing preferences (Gong et al., 2022); However, other studies hold contradictory views, believing that green attributes are not always assets, but may instead be converted into liabilities in certain situations, resulting in negative effects (Skard et al., 2021). Finally, consumers' attentions and sentiments towards different attributes of a product are not always fixed, but rather change accordingly with changes in the environment and product improvement (Shen et al., 2021). Therefore, this study uses the following 3 RQs to guide the research in this paper.

paragraphs: Firstly, in order to ensure the objectivity and rigor of sample selection, this study determined the scope of the study to be four daily consumption areas: organic food, daily cleaning, green building materials, and energy-saving appliances, based on the survey results of the "2021 China Sustainable Consumption Report" (JIEMIAN.com & SynTao-Sustainability Solutions, 2021). This report is jointly released by two influential consulting companies in China that specialize in original news and self-media. Secondly, for the selection of specific products, the study referred to the "List of green product evaluations standards and certification catalogue" published by the State Administration for Market Regulation of the People's Republic of China (State Administration for Market Regulation of the People's Republic of China, 2021) and the "List of g reen design products in 2021″ published by the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology of the People's Republic of China (Ministry of Industry and Information Technology of the People's Republic of China, 2022). Finally, while considering the availability and quality of data, the specific products were identified as 8 green products in residents' daily lives: organic milk, phosphorus-free detergents, energy-saving LED lights, eco-friendly latex paint, eco-friendly wood flooring, solar water heaters, mute washing machines, and energy-saving air conditioner. The research objects and sub-categories of this paper are shown in Table 1.

sections.paper_id: Li et al. - 2023 - How to win the green market Exploring the satisfaction and sentiment of Chinese consumers based on
paragraphs: In this section, the paper chooses the online reviews of 8 typical green products from JD.com as the primary data source. The JD.com is one of the popular and influential e-commerce websites in the field of ecommerce in China, selling millions of high-quality products from 12 categories, including home appliances, digital communication, computers, home furnishings, clothing, mother and baby, books, food, and online tourism. This paper chooses Jingdong Mall's (JD.com) online reviews as the main data source for the following three reasons: (1)The JD.com is the largest B2C mall platform in China, which can provide consumers with a very rich product selection and convenient shopping experience, fully meeting the requirements for sample category selection in this paper; (2)The JD.com has the highest quality self -built logistics service system and obvious advantages in product quality, aftersales service, financial integrity, etc., which has a great influence; (3) The JD.com Mall platform itself has a very high traffic exposure and a complete customer evaluations mechanism (Duan et al., 2022), which gives it greater advantages in both the quality and quantity of online reviews. An example of the online reviews data on JD.com for this paper is shown in Fig. 2.

This study used Octopus Collector (Octoparsev8.5.2) to collect online reviews data. The Octopus Collector can simulate and record human behavior while browsing web pages, capture and store pre-set key information, and meet the need for automated collection of target web page data by continuously repeating a series of preset actions. In the end, the paper acquired a total of 110000 pieces of online reviews from 8 typical green products, with the reviews data from January 1, 2020, to May 20, 2022. The acquired fields involve several aspects, including member ID, star rating of reviews, content, time, product attributes, page URLs, and review types. And the collected online reviews were stored in Excel form with the product link as the storage unit.

According to previous research, online reviews have become an important source for consumers to obtain product or service information (Farivar et al., 2018). The content contained in online reviews not only supplement information about products, services, and shopping experiences, but also effectively reduce consumers' cognitive and search costs, helping them make more reasonable purchasing decisions (Guo et al., 2020). Due to the rules of e-commerce platforms, online reviews are often more reliable than that information obtained through traditional questionnaires or interviews, and are more likely to gain the trust of potential consumers (Ismagilova et al., 2020). Moreover, it can provide immediate information feedback and more accurately reflect customers' needs and preferences, which not only provides a large amount of useful consumer demand preference information for enterprise marketers, but also provides rich research materials for academic research (Danner & Menapace, 2020).

In previous studies, some scholars have formed rich achievements in studying consumer perspectives, sentiments, intentions, and satisfactions by mining online reviews (Ibrahim & Wang, 2019;Mariani & Borghi, 2020). paragraphs: In the process of collecting data, due to the large workload and frequent operations, it is inevitable to collect some duplicate, incorrect, and invalid data during the data crawling process, making the original data obtained on the page non-standard. Therefore, before conducting subsequent analysis, this paper referred to previous research (Wang et al., 2022) and used Python 3.10 to clean the 110000 original reviews captured, removing data that were duplicate, incorrect, invalid, and meaningless to avoid affecting subsequent analysis. It mainly includes:

(1) Eliminating duplicate reviews: reviews with the same user nickname, the same reviews content, or the same or similar comment time. (2) Eliminating invalid reviews: reviews with content unrelated to products; reviews composed of emoticons and special characters. (3) Reviews with low reference value: system default reviews, e.g., "This user thinks the product is good," "This user did not fill in the review in time,"; reviews with less than 10 words; Reviews with confusing format and defective content; reviews that are too colloquial or composed of meaningless modal particles. After eliminating, the study got 85,306 online reviews of green products and Table 2 compares the number of online reviews and data efficiency before and after the elimination.

paragraphs: Usually, the online reviews posted by consumers on e-commerce platforms are more colloquial, without a standardized structure or unified format. Therefore, before conducting subsequent analysis, further processing of the data is required, including word segmentation and de stopping words, to transform them into data forms that can be recognized by machines. This research refers to the previous research (Huang, Long, Chen, Sun, & Li, 2022) to segment and remove stop words from the cleaned reviews.

paragraphs: Sentiment analysis is also known as opinion mining (Liu & Hu, 2019). It is a process that uses natural language processing, text analysis, and computational linguistics to systematically identify, extract, quantify, summarize, and analyze subjective texts with sentimental overtones and to discern the sentimental overtones of customers and thereby deduce their sentimental tendencies (Brzustewicz & Singh, 2021). At present, the methods of sentiment analysis mainly include dictionary-based and rule-based methods, as well as methods based on statistical machine learning (Zhou et al., 2022). The paper mainly uses the SonwNLP class library, a natural language processing component in Python, to process the text content. The SonwNLP library has a training corpus which can classify text into positive and negative categories through Naive Bayesian Classifier. The closer the return value is to 1, the more positive the text content is, and the closer the return value is to 0, the more negative the text content is.

paragraphs: Time series data can reflect the dynamic characteristics of the described phenomenon over time. Consumers' sentiments orientation and trends towards green products can be reflected in consumers' attentions, positive and negative sentiments, and their changing trends for each attribute of green products. The more frequently an attribute appears in online reviews, the more attentions and importance consumers attach to that attribute. The fact that consumers have positive sentiments towards the attribute indicates that consumers are more satisfied with the attribute. For example, when consumers pay more attention to the green attributes of products, consumers attach more importance to green attributes. When consumers' positive sentiments towards green attributes is high and keeps increasing, the product's green attributes will be continuously recognized by consumers. It means that there is still much room for improvement in the green attributes of the product. In order to better understand consumers' attentions to various attributes of green products and the trend of positive and negative sentiments, this paper refers to the previous research (Shen et al., 2021) and adopts the Mann-Kendall method to analyze the relevant attributes of green products. The Mann-Kendall trend test is a non-parametric test method proposed by Mann and Kendall (Kendall, 1975;Mann, 1945), also known as the distribution-free test. It is suitable for analyzing time series with continuous increasing or decreasing trends (monotonic trends) whose advantage is sample data does not need to satisfy the assumption of normal distribution, nor is it disturbed by individual outliers. Therefore, it is widely used in academic research related to trend testing.
For a time series X, the statistic S in the Mann-Kendall test can be defined as (Kendall, 1975;Mann, 1945):
Among them, n represents the total number of time series data points, x j represents the previous data point, and x i represents the current data point.

The following formula can be obtained by standardizing the statistic S: The statistic Z obeys a standard normal distribution. If the p-value is less than the significance level (α = 0.05), it indicates a significant increasing or decreasing trend.

paragraphs: In order to fully understand consumers' attentions and sentiments towards each attribute of existing green products and to explore the critical information in online reviews, this study first needs to identify the product attributes involved in online reviews, i.e., which aspects of products consumers focus on in their online reviews. In order to ensure the effectiveness of word frequency analysis, in the actual processing process, words with a length of 1 and words without actual meaning in subsequent analysis, e.g., "very", "JingDong", "special", "special", etc., have been deleted. Finally, save the Chinese word segmentation results in a table and create a word frequency analysis chart for the first 20 words. The word frequency analysis results for each product in this study are shown in Fig. 3a-h.

From the perspective of organic food consumption, according to Fig. 3a, it is evident that "organic" is the most frequently used term in the online reviews of organic milk (Vargas Meza & Yamanaka, 2020), indicating that consumers can clearly pay attention to the environmental attributes of the product. Next is "packaging", which indicates whether the packaging of organic milk can be intact during transportation, which is a very important aspect of its concern. Then, there are attributes, e.g., "smell", "taste", "date". Smell and taste are important characteristics to measure the quality of organic food, and are also important factors for consumer decision-making and evaluations of organic food (Chekima et al., 2017). These factors all rank before the price, indicating that compared to the "price" of the product, consumers are more concerned about whether the product is healthy and fresh, and how it tastes. In addition, "child" is also a relatively frequent term, indicating that many consumers who purchase organic milk are parents with children. Through observation of online reviews, this research has found that the term "child" often appears in expressions, e.g., "children love to drink" or "children love to drink", indicating that whether children like it is an important factor for parents to consider purchasing organic milk (Zhang et al., 2018).

In the field of daily cleaning consumption, it can be seen from Fig. 3b that "smell" is the most frequently used word in the online reviews of phosphorus-free detergents, and in addition to "smell", there are expressions, e.g., "fragrance" and "pleasant smell", indicating that this is the aspect that consumers are most concerned about. After "smell" are "price" and "performance", which are usually the most intuitive product attributes that consumers feel when purchasing and using detergents, and therefore become some of the most important factors affecting their satisfactions. It is worth noting that although phosphorus-free detergents are a representative green product, descriptions related to their green environmental attributes, e.g., "phosphorus-free" and "water saving", rarely appear in these reviews.

From the perspective of green building materials consumption, it can be seen from Fig. 3c cthat the five factors that consumers are most concerned about energy-saving LED lights are "quality", "brightness", "brand", "energy-saving", and "price". Consumers of eco-friendly latex paint focus on the "quality", "price", "logistics", "packaging", and "effect" of the product, consumers of eco-friendly wooden flooring are more concerned about "quality", "materials", "cost performance", "style", and "odor", can be seen from Fig. 3d and e. And both two products' consumers have mentioned the "environmental" attributes of the product. Although compared to other words, "environmental" appear less frequently, However, through observation of the reviews, it can be found that "eco-friendly" often appears together with "odorous" and "odorless", indicating that consumers' attentions to "odor" is a disguised concern for environmental attributes. In order to fully understand consumers' attentions and sentiments towards each attribute of existing green products and to explore the critical information in online reviews, this study first needs to identify the product attributes involved in online reviews, i.e., which aspects of products consumers focus on in their online reviews. In order to ensure the effectiveness of word frequency analysis, in the actual processing process, words with a length of 1 and words without actual meaning in subsequent analysis, e.g., "very", "JingDong", "special", "special", etc., have been deleted. Finally, save the Chinese word segmentation results in a table and create a word frequency analysis chart for the first 20 words. The word frequency analysis results for each product in this study are shown in Fig. 3a-h.

From the perspective of organic food consumption, according to Fig. 3a, it is evident that "organic" is the most frequently used term in the online reviews of organic milk (Vargas Meza & Yamanaka, 2020), indicating that consumers can clearly pay attention to the environmental attributes of the product. Next is "packaging", which indicates whether the packaging of organic milk can be intact during transportation, which is a very important aspect of its concern. Then, there are attributes, e.g., "smell", "taste", "date". Smell and taste are important characteristics to measure the quality of organic food, and are also important factors for consumer decision-making and evaluations of organic food (Chekima et al., 2017). These factors all rank before the price, indicating that compared to the "price" of the product, consumers are more concerned about whether the product is healthy and fresh, and how it tastes. In addition, "child" is also a relatively frequent term, indicating that many consumers who purchase organic milk are parents with children. Through observation of online reviews, this research has found that the term "child" often appears in expressions, e.g., "children love to drink" or "children love to drink", indicating that whether children like it is an important factor for parents to consider purchasing organic milk (Zhang et al., 2018).

In the field of daily cleaning consumption, it can be seen from Fig. 3b that "smell" is the most frequently used word in the online reviews of phosphorus-free detergents, and in addition to "smell", there are expressions, e.g., "fragrance" and "pleasant smell", indicating that this is the aspect that consumers are most concerned about. After "smell" are "price" and "performance", which are usually the most intuitive product attributes that consumers feel when purchasing and using detergents, and therefore become some of the most important factors affecting their satisfactions. It is worth noting that although phosphorus-free detergents are a representative green product, descriptions related to their green environmental attributes, e.g., "phosphorus-free" and "water saving", rarely appear in these reviews.

paragraphs: In the previous analysis, the current study only analyzed the top 20 words with the highest frequency of occurrence. In fact, these consumers used a greatly abounding language expression in these products' online reviews. To avoid neglecting the secondary attributes of these products and provide more comprehensive evaluations, this part will use word cloud analysis to present the online reviews of these products more intuitively. Meanwhile, based on the results of word frequency analysis, this study summarized online reviews of products belonging to the same consumption field before drawing a word cloud map. In the word cloud analysis, the font of each word is proportional to the frequency of the word. The higher the frequency, the higher the weight of the word, and the larger the size of the word. Fig. 4 is a word cloud diagram drawn based on the results of online reviews analysis in four consumption fields. On the one hand, it can intuitively see that consumers use various expressions in online reviews of these products, including multiple aspects of green products form Fig. 4, e.g., "price", "packing", "function", "quality", "appearance", "logistics", "date", "material", etc. These terms related to product attributes convey Chinese consumers' comprehensive and various feedback on these products, which are key factors affecting the final sentimental tendencies of historical consumers. On the other hand, Fig. 4 also vividly displays consumers' positive and negative sentiments towards existing green products, e.g., "very good", "not bad", "satisfied", "trustworthy", "repurchase" etc.

paragraphs: Through word frequency analysis and word cloud analysis of online reviews, it can initially determine that consumers' overall evaluations of these green products are positive. However, further calculation of the sentiment value of each review is needed to precisely understand the sentiment probability contained in the online reviews. The paper refers to the previous research (Zhou et al., 2022), and conducts analysis on the sentiment probability of the reviews of these 8 products by invoking the SnowNLP natural language processing component in Python. Fig. 5 is a pie chart of the sentimental distribution based on the sentiment analysis results of each product. According to Fig. 5, it is evident that the number of reviews containing positive sentiments is much greater than the number of reviews containing negative sentiments. This indicates that consumers tend to have positive overall sentiments orientation towards these green products.

paragraphs: In this paper, when classifying the affective polarity of reviews, the paper refers to the method by Zhou et al. (2022) and take 0.4 as the limit C. Li et al. of affective polarity, with affective probability above 0.4 being positive and regarded as favorable reviews and below 0.4 being negative and regarded as unfavorable reviews (Zhou et al., 2022). After obtaining the sentiment polarity of each review, the paper measures the ratio of the number of positive reviews to the number of total reviews to get the consumers' positive feedback rate, or satisfaction for each product. According to the result of Table 3, the product with the highest consumer satisfaction is eco-friendly wooden flooring, which has a positive feedback rate of 87.97%. Next is the energy-saving LED lights, with a positive feedback rate of 81.42%. Solar water heaters and eco-friendly latex paint ranked last, with a positive feedback rate of 62.78% and 64.33% respectively. In addition to these products, the positive feedback rate of the rest is maintained at over 70%, indicates that the consumers' overall evaluations and satisfactions with the 8 green products are relatively high.

paragraphs: In order to further determine which factors or attributes of green products are related to consumers' positive and negative sentiments, this study summarized the positive and negative reviews of these 8 products based on the results of sentiment analysis. Then, this study refers to the previous research (Huang, Long, Chen, Sun, & Li, 2022), and introduces the Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) topic model to extract topics related to consumers' positive/negative sentiments. The LDA topic model, a three-layer Bayesian probability model with a word-document-topic structure, has been widely applied to natural language processing since it was proposed by David M. Blei et al., in 2003(Blei et al., 2003). It assumes that each document contains multiple topics. Each topic is a probability distribution based on words and is an unsupervised learning model that can identify potential topics in documents. The key to the LDA topic model is to determine the appropriate number of topics. This study refers to previous study by Brzustewicz and Singh (2021) to select the appropriate number of topics based on the topic perplexity index. The lower the perplexity, the better the results of the model. When the number of potential topics of positive and negative reviews is determined to be 9 and 3 respectively, the extraction result of LDA topic model is optimal.

Table 4 shows the results of topic extraction for positive and negative reviews and the top 20 feature words with the highest frequency under this topic. And the main potential topics related to positive consumer sentiments include: enthusiasm for service, beautiful appearance, good taste, good quality and material, favorable price, trustworthy brand, powerful function and effect, fast logistics and delivery, and high-cost performance; while the underlying topics related to negative sentiments are mainly: poor service attitude, smelly and noisy.

Figs. 6 and 7 are the visual display of the LDA topic extraction for positive and negative reviews. The numbers inside the circles on the left side of Figs. 6 and 7 represent the related topics, and the numbers and size of the circles reflect the probability of each topic appearing. When hovering the mouse over the related topic, the color of the circle corresponding to the topic will turn red accordingly. Currently, the top 30 words with the highest frequency under the topic are displayed on the right side. The blue bar area corresponding to each word after each word represents the frequency of appearance of that word in the whole document, while the red bar area indicates the weight of appearance of that word under the current topic. Fig. 6 shows the visual results of topic 5 after topic extraction on positive reviews. Related feature words under Topic 5 mainly include "price," "affordable," " activity," "favorable," "price ratio," "special offer," "coupons," and other related expressions, indicating that the topic is mainly related to the price. The expression of " favorable price " is a potential topic closely related to the positive sentiments of consumers. Fig. 7 is a visual display of topic 1 in the negative reviews, which has the largest circle, indicating that topic 1 is one of the most critical factors that cause consumers to give negative evaluations on the 8 green products. According to the related keywords under this topic, e.g., "customer service," "logistics," "service," "after-sales," "merchants," "attitude," "service attitude," and other related words, which explained that this is a potential topic related to consumer service.

In order to further determine which factors or attributes of green products are related to consumers' positive and negative sentiments, this study summarized the positive and negative reviews of these 8 products based on the results of sentiment analysis. Then, this study refers to the previous research (Huang, Long, Chen, Sun, & Li, 2022), and introduces the Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) topic model to extract topics related to consumers' positive/negative sentiments. The LDA topic model, a three-layer Bayesian probability model with a word-document-topic structure, has been widely applied to natural language processing since it was proposed by David M. Blei et al., in 2003(Blei et al., 2003). It assumes that each document contains multiple topics. Each topic is a probability distribution based on words and is an unsupervised learning model that can identify potential topics in documents. The key to the LDA topic model is to determine the appropriate number of topics. This study refers to previous study by Brzustewicz and Singh (2021) to select the appropriate number of topics based on the topic perplexity index. The lower the perplexity, the better the results of the model. When the number of potential topics of positive and negative reviews is determined to be 9 and 3 respectively, the extraction result of LDA topic model is optimal.

Table 4 shows the results of topic extraction for positive and negative reviews and the top 20 feature words with the highest frequency under this topic. Finally, the online reviews are converted into structured data related to each product attribute.

Table 6 shows the trend changes in attentions, positive sentiments, and negative sentiments of 8 green product attributes, including "price", "quality" etc., using the Mann-Kendall trend test methods.

This study selected five attributes: "green", "price", "functional effect", "quality", and "cost performance" to conduct a specific analysis of their consumer attentions. Fig. 8 shows the changing trend of consumers' attentions to these five attributes. Moreover, the order in which consumers pay attention to these attributes is price, quality, functional effect, green, and cost performance. This indicates that compared to other attributes, price is the most concerned attribute for consumers. Secondly, the quality and functional effect of products are also factors that attract consumers' attentions more than green attributes. The green attributes and cost performance are basically the same.

According to Fig. 9, there is a significant change in the positive sentiments of consumer towards the green attributes, and although the positive sentimental process of consumers has declined, the overall trend remains long-term growth. The negative sentiments of consumers towards green attributes have relatively little change compared to positive sentiments. Consumers have gone through a process of relatively not recognizing the green attributes of green products to gradually recognizing them. Over time, consumers' satisfactions with green attributes have increased. Fig. 10 shows the changes in consumers' positive and negative sentiments towards 8 attributes of green products in January 2020, January 2021, and January 2022. Li et al. - 2023 - How to win the green market Exploring the satisfaction and sentiment of Chinese consumers based on
paragraphs: Understanding consumers' opinions and evaluations of green products is a very important research field in promoting green consumption. Consumers' sentiments and overall evaluations of these products can be shaped by their online reviews and feedback posted on e-commerce platforms. Therefore, this study integrates multiple online reviews analysis methods and constructs a comprehensive green product online reviews analysis framework. This study not only examined consumers' overall evaluations and sentiments towards these green products, but also focused on which attributes they value more when evaluating green products, and which attributes play a positive role in predicting consumers' positive/negative sentiments.

According to the results of this paper, the online reviews of these 8 green products include consumers' comprehensive and diverse attentions and feedback on different product attributes, mainly including: price, packaging, quality, function, service, cost performance, and green etc. And there are significant differences in the frequency and order of these expressions related to product attributes, reflecting consumers' preferences and trade-offs between different attributes (Luchs & Kumar, 2017). In previous studies, price was often considered a key factor affecting consumers' green consumption decisions (Jo & Shin, 2017), which can significantly regulate the relationship between green attributes and purchase intention (Schuitema & de Groot, 2015). In previous studies , Huang et al. (2022) emphasized the importance of paying attention to the negative sentiment of the public towards green product prices, also provided good evidence for this viewpoint. The research results of this paper support this statement. On the one hand, based on the word frequency analysis, consumers place great importance on the price attributes of the products (Jo & Shin, 2017). On the other hand, from the results of time series analysis, consumers are very concerned about the price attributes of green products, especially the negative sentiments towards prices, which become very prominent and concentrated over time. This once again demonstrates the significance of price among these product attributes (Zhang et al., 2018).

But in different types of products, price is not always the primary factor that consumers value. For example, according to the frequency analysis of organic milk online reviews, this group of consumers seems to value the organic, healthy, taste, and quality of organic milk more than price (Chekima et al., 2017). When products exhibit excellent performance in organic, health, quality, and functionality, consumers are willing to purchase green products and pay a premium for this (Lakatos et al., 2021;Singh & Glińska-Neweś, 2022). According to the frequency analysis of energy-saving home appliance products, consumers of these three products place more emphasis on installation and functional effects compared to their prices. This is not entirely consistent with the previous research results of Malekpour Koupaei et al., 2020, who advocated that consumers place more emphasis on the functional attributes of the product and less on the installation attributes. The reason for this difference may be that in our study, installation emphasized installation services, while in their study, installation emphasized whether the product was easy to install. According to the frequency analysis of green building materials, consumers place more emphasis on quality, packaging, energy conservation, brand, service, logistics and other attributes compared to price.

Previous literature has shown that green attributes are often overlooked when consumers weigh different product attributes comprehensively (Schuitema & de Groot, 2015). The previous research by Cai et al. (2017) supports this viewpoint, arguing that most respondents who purchase furniture value the brand and price of the furniture more, and that green attributes are completely unimportant. The study by Malekpour Koupaei et al. (2020) also confirms this point. Their research on topic extraction for online reviews shows that energy conservation and cost savings are the least discussed topic types. In our current study, the fact is not entirely the case. On the one hand, Chinese consumers' attention and positive sentiments towards green attributes have significantly increased in the past three years. On the other hand, according to the results of word frequency and word cloud analysis, except for consumers who rarely pay attention to the green characteristics of phosphorus-free detergents (Jo & Shin, 2017), most consumers of products are very concerned about the products' green attributes, e.g., organic, energy-saving, eco-friendly, mute, etc. These green attributes appear at the top of the consumer attention list. Especially for consumers of organic milk, the green attribute of "organic" is most discussed in online consumer reviews (Vargas Meza & Yamanaka, 2020).

Moreover, this study also found that consumers' preference for green attributes varies depending on product category (Dinh et al., 2021;Gong et al., 2022). For example, consumers of organic food, green building materials, and energy-saving appliances have a higher preference for green attributes, while consumers of phosphorus-free detergents have a lower preference for green properties. The reasons for this difference may be related to the following four points: (1) whether the green attribute information is clear. For example, in China, products, e.g., energy-saving appliances have clear energy efficiency labels and green attribute information on their appearance. It helps to effectively convey green information to consumers (Zhang et al., 2018). The appearance or outer packaging of phosphorus-free detergents are not labeled with green attributes, and manufacturers rarely market this, resulting in consumers not paying attention to its green attributes (Pancer et al., 2017). ( 2) Is it well-known to consumers. The green attribute terms related to the former, e.g., organic, eco-friendly, and energy conservation, are well-known to consumers (Zhang et al., 2018). The green attribute terms related to the latter, e.g., phosphorus free and bleach-free, are not very common in consumers' life. (3) Is it easy for consumers to perceive. For example, the green attribute of organic food is related to personal health, the green attribute of energy-saving air conditioner is directly related to consumers' cost of living (Malekpour C. Li et al. Koupaei et al., 2020), which is easy to be perceived by consumers, but the green attribute of phosphorus free detergents is related to environmental benefits, with weak effect and slow process, which is not easy to be perceived by consumers. This positive perception and recognition of the value of green attributes (environmental benefits and cost of living savings) is an important reason for this difference (Flores & Jansson, 2021) (4) whether it is related to core functions. The correlation between the green properties of phosphorus-free detergents (e.g., phosphorus -free) and their core functional properties is relatively small (Gong et al., 2022), and they are only by-products developed by Fig. 10. Changes in positive and negative sentiments towards attributes related to green products. companies to improve product functionality (Newman et al., 2014), often directly overlooked by consumers.

According to the results of sentimental analysis, Chinese consumers have a relatively high level of positive evaluations and satisfaction with green products, with 3/4 of the products reaching over 70% satisfaction, and the remaining 1/4 of the products also reaching 60% satisfaction. This is basically consistent with the previous research. In their analysis, the consumers' view of organic food is mostly positive sentiments (Singh & Glińska-Neweś, 2022;Vargas Meza & Yamanaka, 2020).

Based on current research on the extraction of topics related to positive and negative sentiments of consumers, after purchasing green products, consumers' perception and evaluations of these product attributes (e.g., price, cost performance, green, quality, logistics delivery, smell, appearance, and consumer services etc.) all have positive predictive effects on predicting consumer sentiments and satisfactions, i.e., consumer sentiments and satisfactions are influenced by multiple product attributes. This discovery supports previous research. In previous study, consumer satisfactions are influenced by drainage mode, load type, frequency conversion, type, display, color, and capacity (Wang et al., 2018), in Liu and Hu's (2022) study, price, quality, logistics, service and information quality all have a significant impact on consumer satisfactions and loyalty. And in other research, the scholars argue that consumers' perception and evaluations of nutritional composition, ecological welfare, and price will affect their overall evaluations of product functionality, and their perception of appearance, odor, taste, and price will affect consumers' sentimental satisfactions and sensory experiences (Qi & Ploeger, 2021).

Furthermore, it is worth noting that previous studies have emphasized the significant impact of quality perception and value perception of green attributes on green satisfactions (Pahlevi & Suhartanto, 2020), and the increase in green attributes will bring about an overall increase in emotional value through the increase in green sentimental value (Gong & Sheng, 2023). However, the research results of this paper indicate that green products perform poorly in terms of green attributes (mute and noise-free), leading to negative sentiments and dissatisfactions among consumers towards green products.

On the one hand, Chinese consumers' attention and positive sentiments towards green attributes have significantly increased in the past three years. On the other hand, according to the results of word frequency and word cloud analysis, except for consumers who rarely pay attention to the green characteristics of phosphorus-free detergents (Jo & Shin, 2017), most consumers of products are very concerned about the products' green attributes, e.g., organic, energy-saving, eco-friendly, mute, etc. These green attributes appear at the top of the consumer attention list. Especially for consumers of organic milk, the green attribute of "organic" is most discussed in online consumer reviews (Vargas Meza & Yamanaka, 2020).

Moreover, this study also found that consumers' preference for green attributes varies depending on product category (Dinh et al., 2021;Gong et al., 2022). For example, consumers of organic food, green building materials, and energy-saving appliances have a higher preference for green attributes, while consumers of phosphorus-free detergents have a lower preference for green properties. The reasons for this difference may be related to the following four points: (1) whether the green attribute information is clear. For example, in China, products, e.g., energy-saving appliances have clear energy efficiency labels and green attribute information on their appearance. It helps to effectively convey green information to consumers (Zhang et al., 2018). The appearance or outer packaging of phosphorus-free detergents are not labeled with green attributes, and manufacturers rarely market this, resulting in consumers not paying attention to its green attributes (Pancer et al., 2017). ( 2) Is it well-known to consumers. The green attribute terms related to the former, e.g., organic, eco-friendly, and energy conservation, are well-known to consumers (Zhang et al., 2018). The green attribute terms related to the latter, e.g., phosphorus free and bleach-free, are not very common in consumers' life. (3) Is it easy for consumers to perceive. For example, the green attribute of organic food is related to personal health, the green attribute of energy-saving air conditioner is directly related to consumers' cost of living (Malekpour C. Li et al. Koupaei et al., 2020), which is easy to be perceived by consumers, but the green attribute of phosphorus free detergents is related to environmental benefits, with weak effect and slow process, which is not easy to be perceived by consumers. This positive perception and recognition of the value of green attributes (environmental benefits and cost of living savings) is an important reason for this difference (Flores & Jansson, 2021) (4) whether it is related to core functions. The correlation between the green properties of phosphorus-free detergents (e.g., phosphorus -free) and their core functional properties is relatively small (Gong et al., 2022), and they are only by-products developed by Fig. 10. Changes in positive and negative sentiments towards attributes related to green products. companies to improve product functionality (Newman et al., 2014), often directly overlooked by consumers.

According to the results of sentimental analysis, Chinese consumers have a relatively high level of positive evaluations and satisfaction with green products, with 3/4 of the products reaching over 70% satisfaction, and the remaining 1/4 of the products also reaching 60% satisfaction. This is basically consistent with the previous research. In their analysis, the consumers' view of organic food is mostly positive sentiments (Singh & Glińska-Neweś, 2022;Vargas Meza & Yamanaka, 2020).

Based on current research on the extraction of topics related to positive and negative sentiments of consumers, after purchasing green products, consumers' perception and evaluations of these product attributes (e.g., price, cost performance, green, quality, logistics delivery, smell, appearance, and consumer services etc.) all have positive predictive effects on predicting consumer sentiments and satisfactions, i.e., consumer sentiments and satisfactions are influenced by multiple product attributes. This discovery supports previous research. In previous study, consumer satisfactions are influenced by drainage mode, load type, frequency conversion, type, display, color, and capacity (Wang et al., 2018), in Liu and Hu's (2022) study, price, quality, logistics, service and information quality all have a significant impact on consumer satisfactions and loyalty. And in other research, the scholars argue that consumers' perception and evaluations of nutritional composition, ecological welfare, and price will affect their overall evaluations of product functionality, and their perception of appearance, odor, taste, and price will affect consumers' sentimental satisfactions and sensory experiences (Qi & Ploeger, 2021).

Furthermore, it is worth noting that previous studies have emphasized the significant impact of quality perception and value perception of green attributes on green satisfactions (Pahlevi & Suhartanto, 2020), and the increase in green attributes will bring about an overall increase in emotional value through the increase in green sentimental value (Gong & Sheng, 2023). However, the research results of this paper indicate that green products perform poorly in terms of green attributes (mute and noise-free), leading to negative sentiments and dissatisfactions among consumers towards green products.

Understanding consumers' opinions and evaluations of green products is a very important research field in promoting green consumption. Consumers' sentiments and overall evaluations of these products can be shaped by their online reviews and feedback posted on e-commerce platforms. Therefore, this study integrates multiple online reviews analysis methods and constructs a comprehensive green product online reviews analysis framework. This study not only examined consumers' overall evaluations and sentiments towards these green products, but also focused on which attributes they value more when evaluating green products, and which attributes play a positive role in predicting consumers' positive/negative sentiments.

According to the results of this paper, the online reviews of these 8 green products include consumers' comprehensive and diverse attentions and feedback on different product attributes, mainly including: price, packaging, quality, function, service, cost performance, and green etc. And there are significant differences in the frequency and order of these expressions related to product attributes, reflecting consumers' preferences and trade-offs between different attributes (Luchs & Kumar, 2017). In previous studies, price was often considered a key factor affecting consumers' green consumption decisions (Jo & Shin, 2017), which can significantly regulate the relationship between green attributes and purchase intention (Schuitema & de Groot, 2015). In previous studies , Huang et al. (2022) emphasized the importance of paying attention to the negative sentiment of the public towards green product prices, also provided good evidence for this viewpoint. The research results of this paper support this statement. On the one hand, based on the word frequency analysis, consumers place great importance on the price attributes of the products (Jo & Shin, 2017). On the other hand, from the results of time series analysis, consumers are very concerned about the price attributes of green products, especially the negative sentiments towards prices, which become very prominent and concentrated over time. This once again demonstrates the significance of price among these product attributes (Zhang et al., 2018).

But in different types of products, price is not always the primary factor that consumers value. For example, according to the frequency analysis of organic milk online reviews, this group of consumers seems to value the organic, healthy, taste, and quality of organic milk more than price (Chekima et al., 2017). sections.paper_id: Li et al. - 2023 - How to win the green market Exploring the satisfaction and sentiment of Chinese consumers based on
paragraphs: For the government, on the one hand, it is necessary to fully utilize the positive sentiments of the consumer towards green products and increase the promotion of green products, e.g., active science popularization and promotion of green product knowledges can be achieved through television, the internet, social media, etc., to help consumers have a more comprehensive understanding of the positive impact of purchasing green products and establish a good green consumption atmosphere throughout society. On the other hand, the government should also pay attention to consumers' negative sentiments towards green products and formulate reasonable incentive policies. For example, the government can formulate some subsidy measures to address consumers' negative sentiments towards prices. Especially for products with high price sensitivity, e.g., energy-saving appliances, to encourage consumers to actively choose green products.

For enterprises and manufacturers, due to the diversity and complexity of consumer demand, it is necessary to conduct comprehensive market research before conducting green innovation, deeply understand the consumers' attentions and sentimental changes towards different attributes, especially to balance the relationship between green attributes and non-green attributes, allocate production resources reasonably, and reduce the risk of innovation failure. Secondly, collect the evaluations information of historical consumers on existing green products on a regular basis, especially explore the key factors that affect the formation of consumers' positive and negative sentiments, adjust research and development and Marketing plan in a timely manner, and improve consumers' overall evaluations of green products and purchase intention. Finally, increase consumer publicity, release more green information of products through green marketing, and let consumers understand green products more clearly, e.g., long-term cost savings and environmental benefits. Most consumers may be more concerned about price and neglected how much green products can save them. In addition, manufacturers can also mark green attribute labels at appropriate locations on product packaging to help consumers better identify green products.

For e-commerce platforms, firstly, they can provide consumers with specific incentive measures, e.g., point redemption products or coupons, to encourage consumers to actively share information and experiences related to green products. Secondly, they can establish dedicated green consumption areas to display green products that meet relevant environmental or energy-saving standards, quickly and accurately send relevant green products to target consumers, filling the consumers' lack of understanding of green products, and providing consumers with diverse choices. Thirdly, they also need to comprehensively improve service quality, especially in response to logistics delivery, customer service attitude, after-sales service issues, and other aspects. Based on consumers' feedback, timely and proactive improvement and enhancement are needed to comprehensively enhance consumers' overall evaluations of green products.

Li et al. - 2023 - How to win the green market Exploring the satisfaction and sentiment of Chinese consumers based on
paragraphs: This study extends the relevant research on consumers' sentiments towards green products, deepens the current understanding of which attributes consumers attach more importance to green products, by performing text mining and sentiment analysis of different types of green products' online reviews on China's JD.com. It provides a new perspective for the study of public green consumer behaviors. The results indicate that consumers' evaluations of green products involves multiple product attributes (e.g., price, function, quality, eco-friendly, and energy-saving, etc.), and the order of the same attribute's frequency shows significant differences among different green products; Consumers' overall evaluations of current 8 green products show relatively positive sentiments and satisfactions; This study identified 9 topics related to consumers' positive sentiments and 3 topics related to negative sentiments thorough LDA topic extraction analysis; By refining the product attributes involved in these topics, this study also found that consumers' attentions and sentiments towards green products' different attributes have undergone varying degrees of change. Findings from this study can help policy makers, businesses, and researchers to better understand how the consumers view different types of green products and their different attributes. It can also help researchers and developers as well as marketing personnel better grasp the main sentiments generated by Chinese consumers when purchasing green products, thereby better guiding them in product innovation and marketing.

paragraphs: Although this paper adopts a relatively novel perspective and uses a data collection method different from traditional green consumption research (Wei et al., 2021), it is not without limitations. Firstly, considering product categories and data quality, current research only selects 8 typical products to explore consumers' sentiments towards these green products. Although we have selected as many representative products of different types as possible during the research process, it still cannot cover all types of green products and green attributes. Secondly, current research focuses on the impact of different attributes at a single level, while ignoring the interaction effects between different product attributes; Finally, the data for this study mainly comes from JD.com, a transaction-based platform, and it is uncertain whether the current research results are universal on other types of platforms, e.g., social media platforms; In addition, the main customer group of JD.com is Chinese consumers, so it is uncertain whether there are differences in the current research results in other countries or regions in Asia.

sections.text: It is interesting to presume that accepting current existing green products can influence attitudes towards to future innovated green products. This could be important for future research: Firstly, future research can increase more different types of products and discuss them on a broader scale, e.g., green fashion, green cars, green cosmetics, green buildings, etc., which are all areas worth further attention and exploration (Flores & Jansson, 2021;Mohd et al., 2021); Secondly, future research may have some valuable findings by further considering the statistical interaction between attributes based on current research, e.g., price and green (Gong et al., 2022;Wang et al., 2018); Once again, future study can compare whether the sentiments and overall evaluations of online consumer will differ dur to different platform types by selecting representative online transaction and social network platforms (Mariani & Borghi, 2020), e.g., Amazon. Com, Weibo, Twitter, Facebook, YouTube, etc. It will be an interesting direction; Finally, future work can expand the analysis to Asia and other regions, and conduct horizontal comparisons among consumers in different regions, which may produce some unexpected results. In the future, the authors also continue to pay attention to this field and continue to conduct our research at a deeper level.
